summary: This page introduces the distinction between energy signals and power signals, explaining that finite-energy signals have zero average power while finite, nonzero-power signals must have infinite energy. It notes that practical signals are typically energy signals, periodic signals with finite one-period squared magnitude are power signals, and some signals, such as ramps, are neither; the page also includes an exercise about the everlasting exponential and begins the next subsection on deterministic and random signals.

figures.caption: Fig. 1.7 Generation of a periodic signal by periodic extension of its segment of one-period duration.
